summary: Round 2 verification: all three targeted round-1 findings (1, 2, 4) are resolved correctly in commit b22d429, and findings 3/5/6 remain as documented deferrals with no drift. No new regressions introduced by the fixes. Grep invariants hold: `ErrorLog|errorLog` in `app/src/main/kotlin/app/sanctum/machina/crash/` → 0 hits, `kotlinx.coroutines` in `CrashHandler.kt` → 0 hits. Tests still reflect production behaviour — the new `init { refresh() }` in CrashState is compatible with the existing Robolectric setUp()/refresh() pattern, and the new `scrubSingleLine` helper is exercised implicitly by the existing stacktrace and null-message assertions.

verified_fixes.evidence: Explicit `init { refresh() }` block is present. `hasUnresolvedCrash.value` now publishes real disk state immediately after Hilt injection — no more implicit priming contract. `MutableStateFlow(false)` seed is only transient within the constructor body before `init` runs.

`markDismissed()` now calls `dismissedFlag.createNewFile()` directly with no `exists()` pre-check. Return value is ignored (idempotent). Matches task-2.md line verbatim and removes the redundant stat syscall.

`buildCrashRecord` now scrubs both `message` (line 79) and `threadName` (line 80) through the shared private helper `scrubSingleLine(raw)` at lines 95-96, which replaces `\n` and `\r` with spaces. Header single-line invariant for `thread:` is now symmetric with `message:`.

deferred_findings.note: `crashLogWriter` remains `internal val` — acceptable per task hints (`форма на выбор имплементатора`). Deferred as optional polish.

Triplet `LOG_DIR` / `CRASH_LOG` / `DISMISSED_FLAG` still duplicated between the two files. Intentional per task-2 implementation hint (`НЕ выноси файловые пути-константы в публичный API`). Task 8 (Code Audit) owns the consolidation decision.
`context: Context` ctor param not explicitly normalized to `applicationContext` nor KDoc-annotated. Task 5 call site still controls what gets passed. No behavioral bug today.

regression_scan.crashhandler_scrubSingleLine_helper: Private, pure string op (two `replace` calls). No I/O, no allocations beyond the result string. Exercised transitively by `nullMessageException_rendersEmptyMessageNotLiteralNull` (empty-message path) and `writesCrashLogBeforeKiller` (normal-message path). Thread name from `Thread.currentThread().name` in tests is single-line in practice, so normalization is a no-op there — still correctly covered.
regression_scan.crashstate_init_refresh: Added `init { refresh() }` runs on Hilt singleton creation and on manual `CrashState(context)` in tests. `CrashStateTest.setUp()` still calls `logsDir.deleteRecursively()` BEFORE `CrashState(context)` instantiation (line 33-34 in the test), so `init { refresh() }` executes against an empty/nonexistent logs dir and correctly resolves to `false` without exception (matches `freshInstall_noLogsDir_flowFalseNoException`). All tests still call `state.refresh()` explicitly after seeding — semantics preserved.
regression_scan.crashstate_markDismissed_drop_guard: `File.createNewFile()` on an already-existing file returns `false` without throwing (JVM contract). Dropping the pre-check cannot produce new exceptions. `markDismissed_flipsToFalseAndCreatesFlag` still passes semantically (flag file exists + flow == false).

tests_reflect_production.CrashHandlerTest: Unchanged. Still covers: write-before-kill ordering, dismissed-flag deletion, overwrite-not-append, outer-catch-once, >100KB head-truncation + marker, reflective no-suspend shape, and null-message → empty `message:` line. New `scrubSingleLine` helper is exercised implicitly and would need an additional test only if the caller ever fed it a multi-line message or thread name (low-value edge; acceptable to skip).
tests_reflect_production.CrashStateTest: Unchanged and still aligned with the new `init { refresh() }`. Each test explicitly re-calls `state.refresh()` after seeding files, so the `init`-driven refresh (which sees an empty dir due to `deleteRecursively()` right before `CrashState(context)` in setUp) does not interfere. `freshInstall_noLogsDir_flowFalseNoException` continues to exercise the nonexistent-dir path directly.
